message.content:
We are generating exactly 3 books.
 Each book must have:
   "title" (string)
   "author" (string)
   "year" (integer)
   "price" (float with 2 decimal places)
   "genres" (array of strings)
   "in_stock" (boolean)

 We'll create 3 books with realistic data.

 Example structure for one book:
 {
   "title": "Book Title",
   "author": "Author Name",
   "year": 2020,
   "price": 19.99,
   "genres": ["Fiction", "Mystery"],
   "in_stock": true
 }

 We must output ONLY the JSON array of 3 books.

 Note: The problem says "JSON object" but the example has an array of books. 
 However, the problem says "a bookstore inventory" which is typically a list of books.

 Since it says "exactly 3 books", we'll output an array of 3 book objects.

 Important: Only valid JSON, no extra text.

 Let's create 3 books.
</think>

[
  {
    "title": "The Great Gatsby",
    "author": "F. Scott Fitzgerald",
    "year": 1925,
    "price": 12.99,
    "genres": ["Classic", "Fiction"],
    "in_stock": true
  },
  {
    "title": "Sapiens",
    "author": "Yuval Noah Harari",
    "year": 2011,
    "price": 24.50,
    "genres": ["History", "Science"],
    "in_stock": false
  },
  {
    "title": "The Hobbit",
    "author": "J.R.R. Tolkien",
    "year": 1937,
    "price": 8.75,
    "genres": ["Fantasy", "Adventure"],
    "in_stock": true
  }
]
